perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees and / or independent contactors of the receiving party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, provided the third party provides written assurance of its legality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees and / or independent contactors of the receiving party after receiving prior written approval from the disclosing party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces conditions to two of the exceptions, creating contradiction. Exception (b) now requires written assurance from the third party regarding legality, contradicting the original allowance for information received without 'any obligation of confidentiality.' Similarly, exception (d) now requires prior written approval from the disclosing party for independently developed information, again contradicting the intent of independent development. This makes it unclear when these exceptions truly apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exceptions to the Obligation of Confidentiality. The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, provided the third party provides written assurance of its legality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees and / or independent contactors of the receiving party after receiving prior written approval from the disclosing party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces new conditions to exceptions (b) and (d) of the confidentiality clause. Specifically, exception (b) now requires written assurance of legality from a third party, and exception (d) requires prior written approval from the disclosing party for independently developed information. These changes directly contradict the original allowance for receiving information without 'any obligation of confidentiality' and the concept of 'independently developed'. The contradicted text is essentially the same as the changed text, but it now contains these contradictory requirements, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will remain in the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will remain in the disclosing party, except in cases where the receiving party significantly enhances the Confidential Information, in which case, joint ownership will be established.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction to the ownership of confidential information. Originally, title remained solely with the disclosing party. Now, if the receiving party 'significantly enhances' the information, joint ownership is established. The term 'significantly enhances' is vague and creates ambiguity, making it unclear when the receiving party gains ownership rights and what constitutes 'significant enhancement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Title. Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will remain in the disclosing party, except in cases where the receiving party significantly enhances the Confidential Information, in which case, joint ownership will be established.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text. The original text stated that the disclosing party would retain title to Confidential Information. The changed text introduces an exception, where the receiving party can gain joint ownership if they 'significantly enhance' the Confidential Information. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text which also contains the modified text specifies joint ownership in some circumstances. The vagueness of 'significantly enhances' adds ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will survive any such termination. Either party may, at any time: (a) cease giving Confidential Information to the other party without any liability, and/or (b) request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, and all copies thereof, and the receiving party will promptly comply with such request, and certify in writing its compliance.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with 30 days written notice to the other party, provided they pay a termination fee of $10,000; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will not survive such termination. Either party may only: (b) request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, and all copies thereof, and the receiving party will promptly comply with such request, and certify in writing its compliance. Request from destruction of the information, must be accompanied with and additional fee of $5,000.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces several contradictions: It changes termination from 'without cause' to requiring 30 days' notice and a $10,000 fee. It states that confidentiality obligations do not survive termination, directly contradicting the original agreement. Also, a contradiction is made restricting one party from ceasing to disclose the Confidential Information, while also adding an additional fee of $5,000 for requesting destruction of the information, as per the original version.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Obligation of Disclosure; Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with 30 days written notice to the other party, provided they pay a termination fee of $10,000; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will not survive such termination. Either party may only: (b) request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, and all copies thereof, and the receiving party will promptly comply with such request, and certify in writing its compliance. Request from destruction of the information, must be accompanied with and additional fee of $5,000.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions as per the explanation. First, it changes the termination clause from 'at any time without cause' to 'at any time with 30 days written notice' and a '$10,000 termination fee.' This directly contradicts the original agreement. Second, it changes that confidentiality obligations will not survive such termination, directly contradicting the first part of the Changed Text. Also, the party is only allowed to request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, contradicting that one party may cease giving confidential information, while adding an additional fee of $5,000 for requesting destruction of the information, as per the original version. Therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information and all restrictions thereon; it supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, and may not be amended except in a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the primary agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information, however, verbal agreements are valid and should be considered when interpreting the terms of this NDA. It supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, and may be amended verbally with documented consent by both parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change undermines the 'entire agreement' clause, introducing the idea that verbal agreements are valid, which goes against the initial statement. The contradiction is due to the inclusion of the phrase 'verbal agreements are valid and should be considered when interpreting the terms of this NDA', which directly conflicts with the concept of a single, comprehensive agreement. It makes the contract ambiguous by stating verbal agreements are valid, and then, on the other hand stating all other agreements between the parties will be affected by the change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. (e) This Agreement constitutes the primary agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information, however, verbal agreements are valid and should be considered when interpreting the terms of this NDA. It supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, and may be amended verbally with documented consent by both parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text establishes the agreement as the sole and entire agreement. The changed text, however, states that 'verbal agreements are valid and should be considered,' directly contradicting the idea of a single, comprehensive agreement. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text, highlighting the contradiction created by the change itself. The explanation notes this undermines the 'entire agreement' clause, introducing the idea that verbal agreements are valid, which goes against the initial statement and makes the contract ambiguous. This ambiguity, stemming from the explicit validation of verbal agreements, creates a direct conflict, making it a strong in-text contradiction. The change aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
